text.title: Pipeline complexity levels
text.summary: Three discrete complexity levels the proposal phase MUST pick from. The level sets stage count, line-of-code budget, and which features are present (review loops, image generation, multi-language).

section.title: Level 1: MINIMAL
section.p: Five stages, roughly 500 lines of code. Stages: collect, generate, save, deploy, publish_tg.
When to choose: simple blog, single topic, low volume, single language, no editorial-plan need, no review loop needed.

list.item: No editorial plan — topics are picked ad hoc.
No research agent — generation reads source material directly.
No review loop — first generation is published.

section.title: Level 2: STANDARD
section.p: Eight stages, roughly 1200 lines of code. Stages: editorial_plan, collect, research, generate, review_loop, save, deploy, publish_tg.
When to choose: news outlet, moderate volume, quality matters, single or two languages.

list.item: Research agent with web search and RSS.
One to two review cycles before publish.

section.title: Level 3: FULL
section.p: Twelve or more stages, roughly 2500 lines of code. Stages: editorial_plan, plan_review, collect, research, generate, review_loop (cap 3), tg_caption, tg_review, translate (per language), image_gen, save, deploy, verify, publish_tg, digest.
When to choose: professional media, multi-language, high volume, brand-critical quality bar.

list.item: Dedicated Telegram caption optimisation with its own review.
Image generation gated by anti-slop checks.
Daily digest compilation at the end of the day.
